name: Aerospace Engineering
description: Aerospace engineering encompasses the design, development, and testing of both aircraft and spacecraft, covering atmospheric and space flight. It integrates aerodynamics, propulsion, materials, structural analysis, and orbital mechanics to create vehicles that operate from Earth's surface to outer space.

subjects.name: Space Flight Mechanics
subjects.description: Fundamentals of space flight including orbital mechanics, Kepler's laws, orbital elements, and spacecraft trajectories. Covers two-body problem, orbital maneuvers, interplanetary transfers, and orbit determination methods.
learning_outcomes: Apply Kepler's laws and the two-body problem to describe satellite motion
Calculate orbital elements from position and velocity vectors
Design Hohmann transfer and bi-elliptic transfer maneuvers
Perform orbit determination using Gibbs and Lambert methods
subjects.industry_relevance: Fundamental for satellite operations, mission planning, and space debris management.

subjects.name: Rocket Propulsion
subjects.description: Principles of rocket propulsion including solid, liquid, and hybrid rocket motors. Covers thrust chamber design, nozzle theory, propellant chemistry, combustion instability, and rocket performance parameters.
learning_outcomes: Analyze rocket motor performance using characteristic velocity and thrust coefficient
Design solid propellant grain configurations for desired thrust profiles
Evaluate liquid rocket engine cycles including pressure-fed and pump-fed systems
Calculate rocket vehicle staging and payload optimization
subjects.industry_relevance: Core knowledge for launch vehicle development, missile systems, and spacecraft propulsion.

subjects.name: Satellite Technology
subjects.description: Design and operation of artificial satellites including bus subsystems, payload integration, thermal control, power systems, and attitude control. Covers satellite classification, orbital slots, and launch interfaces.
learning_outcomes: Design satellite bus subsystems including power, thermal, and attitude control
Integrate payloads with satellite platforms for specific mission objectives
Analyze satellite thermal balance and design passive and active thermal control
Evaluate satellite attitude determination and control using sensors and actuators
subjects.industry_relevance: Directly applicable to satellite manufacturing companies and space agency programs.

subjects.name: Orbital Dynamics
subjects.description: Advanced orbital mechanics including perturbed orbits, three-body problem, Lagrange points, and relative motion. Covers gravitational perturbations, atmospheric drag, solar radiation pressure, and station-keeping maneuvers.
learning_outcomes: Analyze orbital perturbations due to Earth's oblateness and third-body effects
Design station-keeping strategies for geostationary and LEO satellites
Evaluate three-body problem and Lagrange point orbits for space missions
Apply relative orbital motion equations for rendezvous and formation flying
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for satellite constellation management, deep space mission design, and space situational awareness.

subjects.name: Spacecraft Design
subjects.description: Conceptual and preliminary design of spacecraft including mission definition, configuration design, mass budget, power budget, and structural design. Covers design trade-offs, space environment effects, and spacecraft testing.
learning_outcomes: Develop spacecraft design requirements from mission statement and objectives
Prepare spacecraft mass, power, and data budgets for preliminary design
Design spacecraft structures considering launch loads and space environment
Evaluate spacecraft testing requirements including vibration, thermal vacuum, and EMC tests
subjects.industry_relevance: Culminating design subject preparing students for spacecraft engineering careers.

subjects.name: Launch Vehicle Technology
subjects.description: Design and analysis of launch vehicles including multistage rockets, launch trajectories, stage separation, and payload fairing design. Covers launch vehicle sizing, structural design, aerodynamics of launch, and range safety.
learning_outcomes: Optimize launch vehicle staging for maximum payload to orbit
Design ascent trajectories accounting for gravity losses and aerodynamic loads
Analyze stage separation dynamics and payload fairing jettison
Evaluate launch vehicle structural design for boost phase loads
subjects.industry_relevance: Core to launch vehicle manufacturers and space agencies developing orbital and suborbital launchers.

subjects.name: Atmospheric Flight Mechanics
subjects.description: Flight mechanics of aerospace vehicles during atmospheric flight including ascent, re-entry, and maneuvering. Covers aerodynamic forces, stability and control of launch vehicles, re-entry vehicles, and hypersonic flight dynamics.
learning_outcomes: Analyze re-entry trajectories and aerodynamic heating for Earth return vehicles
Design hypersonic vehicle configurations for controlled atmospheric flight
Evaluate stability and control characteristics of launch vehicles during ascent
Apply heat shield and thermal protection system design for re-entry vehicles
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for re-entry vehicle design, hypersonic research, and planetary entry probe development.

subjects.name: Aerospace Materials
subjects.description: Advanced materials for aerospace applications including lightweight alloys, ceramic matrix composites, carbon-carbon composites, and ablative materials. Covers material behavior in space environment, radiation effects, and thermal protection materials.
learning_outcomes: Select materials for spacecraft and launch vehicle structures considering space environment effects
Evaluate ablative and reusable thermal protection systems for re-entry vehicles
Analyze radiation damage mechanisms in electronic and structural materials
Apply advanced composite manufacturing for lightweight aerospace structures
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for spacecraft manufacturing, thermal protection system design, and materials R&D.

subjects.name: Space Environment
subjects.description: Study of the space environment including vacuum, plasma, radiation belts, micrometeoroids, and space debris. Covers spacecraft-environment interactions, charging effects, single event effects, and environmental mitigation strategies.
learning_outcomes: Characterize the space radiation environment including van Allen belts and solar particle events
Analyze spacecraft charging and electrostatic discharge mitigation techniques
Evaluate micrometeoroid and orbital debris risks to spacecraft
Design environmental shielding for sensitive spacecraft components
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for ensuring spacecraft reliability and mission success in the harsh space environment.

subjects.name: Remote Sensing
subjects.description: Principles of Earth observation and remote sensing from space including optical, thermal infrared, and microwave sensors. Covers radiative transfer, image processing, classification, and applications in environmental monitoring and defense.
learning_outcomes: Understand radiative transfer and atmospheric correction for satellite imagery
Analyze multi-spectral and hyper-spectral data for land cover classification
Apply synthetic aperture radar principles for all-weather Earth observation
Evaluate remote sensing applications in agriculture, urban planning, and disaster management
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing demand for Earth observation data in agriculture, climate monitoring, and national security.

subjects.name: Telemetry & Communication
subjects.description: Spacecraft telemetry, tracking, and command systems including communication link design, modulation schemes, antennas, and ground station operations. Covers data compression, error correction coding, and space communication protocols.
learning_outcomes: Design satellite communication links considering link budget and propagation effects
Implement telemetry data encoding and compression for space applications
Analyze tracking systems including ranging, Doppler tracking, and interferometry
Evaluate ground station antenna systems and RF front-end design
subjects.industry_relevance: Essential for satellite operations, deep space communication, and ground segment engineering.

subjects.name: Space Mission Design
subjects.description: Comprehensive space mission design including mission objectives, orbit selection, spacecraft architecture, launch vehicle selection, cost estimation, and risk assessment. Covers mission lifecycle from concept through disposal with case studies of real missions.
learning_outcomes: Develop space mission concepts of operations from requirements to disposal
Select optimal orbits and spacecraft architectures for given mission objectives
Perform mission cost estimation using parametric and analogous methods
Evaluate mission risk and develop mitigation strategies for space projects
subjects.industry_relevance: Capstone subject that integrates all aerospace disciplines for mission planning roles.

subjects.name: Guidance & Navigation
subjects.description: Guidance, navigation, and control systems for aerospace vehicles including inertial navigation, GPS, star trackers, and guidance algorithms. Covers Kalman filtering, autonomous navigation, and terminal guidance for rendezvous and landing.
learning_outcomes: Design inertial navigation systems using gyroscopes and accelerometers
Implement Kalman filters for spacecraft state estimation and sensor fusion
Develop guidance algorithms for orbital rendezvous and planetary landing
Evaluate autonomous navigation techniques for deep space missions
subjects.industry_relevance: Core technology for autonomous spacecraft, missile guidance, and planetary exploration rovers.

subjects.name: Satellite Communication
subjects.description: Satellite-based communication systems including fixed and mobile satellite services, VSAT networks, satellite broadcasting, and broadband satellite systems. Covers orbital allocations, frequency bands, modulation, multiple access, and link design.
learning_outcomes: Design satellite communication links for fixed and mobile services
Analyze multiple access schemes including FDMA, TDMA, and CDMA for satellite systems
Evaluate satellite network architectures including bent-pipe and regenerative payloads
Calculate link budgets for various satellite communication applications
subjects.industry_relevance: Growing field with applications in broadband connectivity, direct-to-home TV, and IoT connectivity.

subjects.name: Space Robotics
subjects.description: Robotic systems for space applications including planetary rovers, robotic arms, on-orbit servicing, and autonomous manipulation. Covers kinematics, dynamics, control, vision-based navigation, and human-robot interaction for space missions.
learning_outcomes: Design robotic manipulators for on-orbit assembly and servicing tasks
Develop mobility systems for planetary rovers including wheeled and legged configurations
Implement vision-based navigation and obstacle avoidance for autonomous robots
Evaluate human-robot interaction systems for collaborative space missions
subjects.industry_relevance: Critical for planetary exploration, ISS maintenance, and future in-space manufacturing missions.
